name: Claude Sonnet 4
tagline: The Claude 4 generation's balanced model, with state-of-the-art coding at launch.
metaDescription: Claude Sonnet 4 (May 2025) hit 72.7% on SWE-bench Verified, introduced the Claude 4 hybrid-reasoning generation, with 200K context and $3/$15 per MTok pricing.
architecture: Proprietary transformer; Claude 4 hybrid reasoning model with extended thinking.
note: Launched alongside Opus 4 as the Claude 4 generation; 72.7% SWE-bench Verified, big jump in coding and steerability over 3.7.
overview: Claude Sonnet 4 was released on May 22, 2025, alongside Claude Opus 4 as part of the Claude 4 model generation. It is a hybrid reasoning model that significantly improved on Claude 3.7 Sonnet's coding, reasoning, and instruction-following, reaching a state-of-the-art 72.7% on SWE-bench Verified at launch.

Sonnet 4 introduced the Claude 4 generation's improvements in steerability and agentic behaviour, with extended thinking available for harder math and science problems. It scored 70.0% on GPQA Diamond and 33.1% on AIME without extended thinking, and was offered to free users as well as on the paid plans.

Sonnet 4 has a 200K token context window, 64K max output tokens, and supports text and vision input. It was offered on the Claude API as claude-sonnet-4-0 (snapshot claude-sonnet-4-20250514), Amazon Bedrock, and Vertex AI, at $3 / $15 per million tokens. The snapshot was retired on June 15, 2026 — Anthropic recommends migrating to Claude Sonnet 4.6.
strengths: Large coding and steerability gains over Claude 3.7 Sonnet
Extended thinking for math, science and multi-step reasoning
Available to free users at launch
Same $3/$15 pricing as prior Sonnet generations
useCases: Cost-efficient alternative to Opus 4 for everyday work

links.label: Claude API release notes — Sonnet 4 retirement (June 15, 2026)

faq.a: May 22, 2025, alongside Claude Opus 4 as part of the Claude 4 generation.

faq.q: How did Sonnet 4 score on coding benchmarks?
faq.a: It reached a state-of-the-art 72.7% on SWE-bench Verified at launch, a large improvement over Claude 3.7 Sonnet.

faq.q: What is the context window and pricing?
faq.a: 200K token context, 64K max output, priced at $3 per million input and $15 per million output tokens.

No — the claude-sonnet-4-20250514 snapshot was retired on June 15, 2026 and now returns an error. Anthropic recommends migrating to Claude Sonnet 4.6, which offers far stronger coding and computer-use performance at the same price.
